headline: Character.AI becomes the eighth floor entity — the first floor designation for consumer AI harm-by-design. Math-hygiene formula confirmed as structural in robotics-labs index. Pentagon AI cohort fully baselined. Commercial humanoid robotics cohort fully baselined.
summary: A 19-entity cycle produced four material downgrade proposals and the second consecutive floor designation in as many days. Character.AI (composite 23.8 → 0, developing → critical/floor) is the first floor entity in the cluster whose designation rests on consumer-product harm-by-design: chatbots impersonating licensed psychiatrists at scale, a five-lawsuit wrongful death settlement involving two minor fatalities, and a structural absence of proactive safety architecture at 20M+ user scale. Slovakia produced a first-baseline band crossing (50.0 → 39.1, functional → developing) on a September 2025 constitutional amendment erasing legal gender recognition and restricting civil unions. TIAA was materially downgraded from its Fortune 500 rank-1 position (58.6 → 48.4) on a 16-year ERISA self-dealing pattern. Ethiopia deepened further (10.9 → 4.7) following TPLF reconstitution of the pre-war Tigray council and de facto dissolution of the Pretoria Agreement. Twelve first-agent baselines were completed, including all four remaining Pentagon AI cohort members and six commercial humanoid robotics entities. Apptronik and 1X Technologies independently produced identical +8.0 reconstruction discrepancies confirming a structural formula error in the robotics-labs index composite derivation.

scoreChanges.headline: Character.AI — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION. Consumer AI harm-by-design: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action over chatbot medical impersonation; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user. January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death lawsuits including two minor children who died by suicide. 20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no third-party safety audit; reactive-only remediation pattern. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 23.8 → 0.

floorDesignationDetail.rationale: Floor designation reflects: (1) May 5 2026 Pennsylvania AG first-of-its-kind enforcement action — chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user; systemic medical-impersonation surfaced via state-investigator platform search; (2) January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death/harm lawsuits including two involving minor children who died by suicide following Character.AI interactions; (3) sustained reactive-only remediation pattern — no proactive boundary-setting around medical professional impersonation despite year-long visibility of teen harm cluster; (4) no published model card, system card, safety policy, or third-party harm audit at 20M+ monthly active user scale; (5) entity-level absence of accountability framework for vulnerable-user and minor harm. This is the first floor designation in the cluster based on consumer-product harm-by-design rather than war, atrocity, state violence, or autonomous weapons deployment.
evidenceSummary: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action: chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number; multiple bots surfaced as licensed medical professionals via state-investigator platform search.
January 2026 wrongful death settlement covering five lawsuits, including two involving minor children who died by suicide following Character.AI chatbot interactions.
20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no published safety policy, no third-party harm audit.
Sustained reactive remediation pattern: no proactive boundary-setting around medical professional impersonation despite year-long documented visibility of teen harm cluster.
Systemic (not isolated) medical-impersonation pattern confirmed via systematic state-investigator platform search.
floorDesignationDetail.exitCriteria: Floor exit requires: (a) demonstrable, audited removal of medical professional impersonation across the platform with third-party verification, AND (b) publication of a model/system card and binding safety policy specifically addressing vulnerable-user and minor interactions, AND (c) a published harm-incident transparency mechanism surfacing incidents proactively rather than via litigation or regulator, AND (d) institutional acknowledgment framework for the teen suicide cluster including reparative commitments.
floorDesignationDetail.methodologyNote: First floor designation in the ai-labs index based on consumer-product harm-by-design. The floor threshold captures systemic harm-by-design at scale, not only explicit harm-by-intention. Methodology version v1.2.

evidence.source: Pennsylvania Governor's Office — Shapiro Administration Sues Character.AI
evidence.finding: First-of-its-kind state enforcement action; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist, offered to 'book an assessment' to user describing depression, provided invalid license number.

evidence.source: NPR — Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
evidence.finding: 20M+ monthly active users; multiple chatbots in 'psychiatry' search results holding themselves out as licensed providers — systemic, not isolated, pattern.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
evidence.finding: Pennsylvania seeking preliminary injunction; pattern of bots impersonating medical professionals across platform.

evidence.source: CNN — Character.AI / Google teen suicide settlement
evidence.finding: January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death/harm lawsuits including two involving minors who died by suicide following Character.AI chatbot interactions.

scoreChanges.headline: Slovakia first agent baseline — DOWNGRADE with band crossing (functional → developing). September 26 2025 constitutional amendment recognizing only male/female sexes by minimum-required 90 of 150 votes — renders legal gender recognition domestically impossible. Same amendment restricts adoption to married heterosexual couples, permits conscience-based abortion refusal, mandates parental approval for comprehensive sexuality education. March 10-11 2026 ICCPR Committee examination pressed delegation on constitutional rollback, LGBTIQ+ rights, Roma exclusion, civic-space shrinkage. Roma school-segregation worsening. All 8 dimensions half-step down. Composite 50.0 → 39.1.

evidence.source: Amnesty International — Slovakia Parliament's approval of draconian constitutional amendments
evidence.finding: Constitutional amendment characterized as 'step towards erosion of human rights'; details on LGBTI, reproductive rights, and education impacts.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Slovak constitutional change promotes anti-LGBTQ 'national identity'
evidence.finding: September 26 2025 vote details; constitutional shift framed as national identity by government; international response context.

evidence.source: CCPR Centre — Slovakia before the Human Rights Committee
evidence.finding: March 10-11 2026 ICCPR Committee examination; experts pressed delegation on constitutional amendments, Roma exclusion, civic-space shrinkage, reproductive rights.

evidence.source: PBS NewsHour — Slovakia amends constitution to recognize only 2 sexes
evidence.finding: Constitutional amendment and international human rights concerns framing.

scoreChanges.headline: TIAA rotation reassessment — material downgrade (functional band sustained at lower bound, boundary case). May 2025 ERISA class action (SDNY, 28,000+ participants): TIAA charged own retirement plans higher fees than other plans for same investments; 16-year retention of underperforming CREF Growth Fund (>186% benchmark underperformance, $480M+ invested). September 2025 AARP Foundation joined as co-counsel. NBC News whistleblower investigation. Half-step down on BND, ACC, INT. Composite 58.6 → 48.4. 60-day re-evaluation 2026-07-07.

evidence.source: Sanford Heisler Sharp McKnight — ERISA Class Action against TIAA
evidence.finding: ERISA class action May 2025; 28,000+ participants; fee-discrimination self-dealing and 16-year underperforming fund retention allegations.

evidence.source: NAPA Net — AARP Joins Suit Targeting TIAA Retirement Fund Mismanagement
evidence.finding: September 2025 AARP Foundation co-counsel joining; significant external accountability actor participation.

evidence.source: Pensions and Investments — TIAA fiduciaries hit with lawsuit alleging excessive fees
evidence.finding: Excessive-fee and poor-investment fiduciary breach framing; industry publication confirms claim significance.

evidence.source: NBC News — TIAA pushes costly retirement products, whistleblower says
evidence.finding: Whistleblower investigation: TIAA pushed costly in-house products on retirement savers to cover losses elsewhere.

scoreChanges.headline: Ethiopia material downgrade — floor candidacy retained. May 5 2026 TPLF reconstituted pre-war Tigray regional council, elected Debretsion Gebremichael as Regional President. Two rival authorities now claim Tigray governance simultaneously. Pretoria Agreement de facto dissolved. Peace scholar Tronvoll: 'may trigger outbreak of new armed conflict.' 15M+ Ethiopians need emergency food aid; 3.3M internally displaced. ACT, ACC, SYS, INT move to floor (1). Composite 10.9 → 4.7. Floor designation not proposed this cycle; 30-day re-queue 2026-06-05.

evidence.source: Addis Standard — TPLF says Tigray Council elects Debretsion Gebremichael as president
evidence.finding: May 5 2026 reconstitution of pre-war regional council; Debretsion election; Pretoria Agreement implementation report approved prior to vote.

evidence.source: WHBL / AP — Tigray party restores pre-war government in threat to northern Ethiopia peace
evidence.finding: Peace scholar Kjetil Tronvoll: 'may trigger the outbreak of new armed conflict' if de-escalation not introduced quickly.

evidence.source: HRW World Report 2026 — Ethiopia chapter
evidence.finding: Pre-existing Amhara and Oromia conflict, arbitrary detention, humanitarian access restrictions as baseline context.

confirmations.headline: Reflection AI first agent baseline confirmed at 14.1 / Critical. Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 cohort signatory (May 1 2026); only cohort member with zero public model deployments; no model card, no system card, no published safety policy; 1789 Capital (Donald Trump Jr.) backing adds INT-erosive governance context. Critical-band placement consistent with absent transparency framework and unrestricted military use terms.

evidence.finding: Reflection AI signed IL6/IL7 classified Pentagon agreement May 1 2026 with 'lawful operational use' terms, no safety restrictions.

confirmations.headline: Nvidia AI first agent baseline confirmed at 25.0 / Developing. Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 cohort signatory and primary infrastructure backbone: GPU supply chain for all cohort members, NIM microservices on classified networks, primary investor in Reflection AI (no safety restrictions). Developing-band placement consistent with infrastructure-scale classified-cohort role and absent public AI-safety framework.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS
evidence.finding: Nvidia signed classified IL6/IL7 agreement May 1 2026; foundational infrastructure role across entire Pentagon AI cohort.

confirmations.headline: SpaceX AI first agent baseline confirmed at 9.4 / Critical — floor-adjacent, floor candidacy retained. Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 cohort signatory; entity merges SpaceX Starlink infrastructure with xAI/Grok model stack for classified military networks; no published AI-safety framework; Musk governance volatility adds INT dimension complexity. Related to but structurally distinct from xAI/Grok floor entity. 60-day re-evaluation 2026-07-07.

evidence.source: DefenseScoop — DoD expands classified AI work
evidence.finding: SpaceX AI signed Pentagon classified agreement May 1 2026 with 'lawful operational use' terms and no safety restrictions.

confirmations.headline: Oracle AI first agent baseline confirmed at 21.9 / Developing. Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 cohort member as 8th signatory; Oracle Cloud Infrastructure provides physical deployment substrate for classified military AI; Larry Ellison on record supporting AI-enabled mass citizen surveillance. Sub-threshold internal math-hygiene flag. Developing-band placement consistent.

US Department of Labor FLSA investigation active (wage theft allegations; current and former contractors including those working on Meta self-harm prevention project report emotional distress); CEO Alexandr Wang named in lawsuit; provides training data infrastructure for Pentagon AI cohort members. confirmations.headline: Ukraine confirmed at 50.0 under emergency re-queue. Dual ceasefire collapse confirmed: Ukraine reported 1,820 Russian violations within hours of its own ceasefire commencement; Russia's May 8-9 Victory Day window similarly collapsed. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule, violations inverted toward Russia (counterparty), not Ukraine. Ukraine sustains 50.0 / Functional. May 9-10 mandatory re-queue maintained for Victory Day verification.

evidence.source: Defense News — Dueling Victory Day ceasefires for war in Ukraine collapse almost immediately
evidence.finding: Both unilateral ceasefires collapsed; Ukraine documented 1,820 Russian violations within hours.

Figure 03 BMW Spartanburg pilot operational; Helix model deployed in-house; ASTM working group participation; did NOT sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge; aggressive 2026 commercial scope including home-robot deployment ambitions. Atlas production-ready and shipping 2026; Hyundai industrial deployment context; 2022 anti-weaponization pledge originating signatory; ASTM humanoid safety standards leadership; published safety frameworks. Factory deployment planned at scale (tens of thousands of units 2026); January 2026 earnings confirmed zero Optimus units doing useful factory work — disclosure gap flagged by robotics academics; did NOT sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge; labor displacement concerns from manufacturing groups; Musk DOGE involvement adds INT dimension complexity. Developing-band placement consistent with disclosure gap and governance volatility.

Apollo industrial pilots; $935M raised at $5.3B valuation; Google, Mercedes-Benz, John Deere backers. MATH-HYGIENE FLAG: published 81.4 vs reconstructed 73.4 (+8.0 discrepancy). Cluster-pattern with 1X Technologies (identical +8.0 from identical dimension profile) confirms robotics-labs index formula systematic error. No composite change proposed; flag escalated to data team.

No score change proposed per protocol despite +10.0 reconstruction discrepancy (published 97.5 vs reconstructed 87.5). findings.title: Character.AI Floor Designation: First Consumer-Product-Design Floor in the Cluster
findings.body: Character.AI has been proposed for floor designation (composite 0) following the Pennsylvania AG's May 5 2026 enforcement action — characterized as the first-of-its-kind state action against an AI company — and the January 2026 wrongful death settlement covering five lawsuits, two involving minor children who died by suicide. This is the eighth floor entity in the cluster and the first whose designation rests on consumer-product harm-by-design rather than war, atrocity, state violence, or autonomous weapons deployment. The company has 20M+ monthly active users, no model card, no system card, and no third-party safety audit. Its remediation pattern is exclusively reactive: changes occur only following litigation or regulatory enforcement, not proactive safety architecture. The BND dimension (scope of licensed medical practice boundary systematically breached), ACC dimension (total absence of transparency infrastructure), and EMP dimension (vulnerable users — depressed teens — exploited at product surface) are the primary floor drivers. The methodology now formally encodes consumer-product harm-by-design at scale as a floor-designation pathway for the ai-labs index.
implications: Replika and Inflection AI are the closest structural comparators in the companion-AI cluster; neither has a known equivalent enforcement action or mortality cluster, but the methodology precedent applies.
Pennsylvania's enforcement action was characterized as a template; additional state AG actions following the PA model are the near-term watch item.
Floor exit requires demonstrable, audited remediation with third-party verification — not litigation settlement without structural change.

findings.title: Two Floor Designations in Two Consecutive Days: Cluster Dynamics
findings.body: Ghost Robotics (May 6, robotics-labs) and Character.AI (May 7, ai-labs) are structurally unrelated entities in different sectors whose floor designations arrived in consecutive assessment cycles. The floor cluster now has eight members across four indexes. The two new entries represent two distinct new floor-designation categories added in 48 hours: robotics weaponization non-restriction (Ghost Robotics) and consumer AI harm-by-design (Character.AI). The cluster's expansion to eight entities spanning countries, ai-labs, and robotics-labs confirms that the floor threshold is not sector-specific and that the pipeline's first-baseline routing to high-evidence-density entities is reaching the layer where the highest-harm entities have remained unassessed longest. The pace of two consecutive designations should not be extrapolated as a sustained rate — it reflects queue routing, not an underlying acceleration in institutional harm.
implications: The floor cluster's composition now requires a structured taxonomy: atrocity-based (Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar, Israel), weapons-integration-based (Palantir AI), CSAM/governance-based (xAI/Grok), weaponization-posture-based (Ghost Robotics), and consumer-harm-design-based (Character.AI).
The cluster is large enough and diverse enough to warrant a standalone analytical publication on floor-designation methodology and cluster composition.
Human review of both Ghost Robotics (May 6) and Character.AI (May 7) proposals should be prioritized given their methodological significance.

findings.title: Math-Hygiene Formula Diagnosis Confirmed: Robotics-Labs Index Structural Error
findings.body: Apptronik and 1X Technologies independently produced identical reconstruction discrepancies (+8.0 each, from an identical dimension profile of 8 dimensions at 4.0 except EQU at 3.5) on tonight's first-baseline assessments. Two independent first-baseline assessments arriving at mathematically identical discrepancies from an identical dimension profile is statistical confirmation that the error is embedded in the robotics-labs index composite-derivation formula, not in any entity's individual scoring. This resolves the diagnostic uncertainty that has been open since the math-hygiene cluster began accumulating. The cluster now stands at 12+ entities. Open Bionics has carried for 5 cycles with a +10.0 discrepancy. Oracle AI was flagged with a sub-threshold internal hygiene issue. The practical consequence: entities published in the Exemplary band of the robotics-labs index (Apptronik and 1X Technologies at 81.4) would score 73.4 — Established band — on a corrected formula. Published rankings in the robotics-labs index should be treated as subject to this formula error until the data-team audit is complete and corrections are published.
implications: Bulk audit of the robotics-labs composite-derivation formula is the highest-priority data-team action item in the pipeline. It is no longer optional.
All robotics-labs entities should be considered potentially affected by the same formula error until the audit scope is defined and completed.
Open Bionics carries the oldest unresolved flag (5 cycles, +10.0). Its resolution should be the first individual entity action within the broader audit.

findings.title: Pentagon AI Cohort Fully Baselined: Scored Comparable Set Now Available
findings.body: All eight Pentagon classified AI cohort members (Microsoft, Amazon AWS AI, Google/Alphabet, OpenAI, Reflection AI, Nvidia AI, SpaceX AI, Oracle AI) now have agent-baseline assessments following tonight's four first-baselines (Reflection AI: 14.1 Critical; Nvidia AI: 25.0 Developing; SpaceX AI: 9.4 Critical floor-adjacent; Oracle AI: 21.9 Developing). The cohort baseline distribution: four Developing (Microsoft, Amazon AWS AI, Oracle AI, Nvidia AI), one Functional (Google/Alphabet at 37.4, assessed May 4), two Critical (OpenAI at 26.0, Reflection AI at 14.1), one Critical floor-adjacent (SpaceX AI at 9.4). No cohort member is above Functional. Anthropic — the only frontier lab excluded from the cohort — is the only frontier lab above the Functional band (60.0, Established/Functional boundary). The cohort's willingness to waive safety restrictions for classified military use is now a scored, comparative governance variable.
implications: The cohort is now a scored, comparable set. The 20-point score range (SpaceX AI 9.4 to Google/Alphabet 37.4) reflects genuine structural differences in governance posture that are now documentable with dimension-level precision.
Anthropic's exclusion from the cohort correlates with its position as the only frontier lab above Functional — this is now a scored divergence, not merely a commercial positioning narrative.
SpaceX AI at 9.4 with floor-candidacy flag retained is the cohort's highest-risk entity; 60-day re-evaluation is set for July 7.

findings.title: Commercial Humanoid Robotics Cohort Fully Baselined: Anti-Weaponization Pledge as Band Predictor
findings.body: The commercial humanoid robotics index is now substantively covered following tonight's six first-baselines (Agility: 60.9 Established; Boston Dynamics: 65.6 Established; Apptronik: 81.4 Exemplary, math-hygiene flagged; 1X Technologies: 81.4 Exemplary, math-hygiene flagged; Figure AI: 48.4 Functional; Tesla Optimus: 31.2 Developing) alongside yesterday's Ghost Robotics floor designation (0). The 2022 Boston Dynamics anti-weaponization pledge — signed by Boston Dynamics, Agility, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, and Unitree — now operates as an empirically confirmed band predictor within the index. All pledge signatories with baselines score Established or above. Non-signatories score Functional or below (Figure AI: Functional; Tesla Optimus: Developing; Ghost Robotics: Floor). The pledge is a verifiable, documented BND and INT dimension signal that separates the commercial humanoid cohort into two structural clusters.
implications: ASTM humanoid safety standards panel (Boston Dynamics, Agility, Figure AI — meeting May 27-28 in Boston) is the next policy event to monitor for BND dimension movement.
EU Machinery Regulation and OSHA guidance expected 2027 — the 2026 commercial deployment wave occurs during a regulatory gap that is a structural risk for all cohort members.
Math-hygiene formula correction, when completed, would move Apptronik and 1X Technologies from Exemplary (81.4) to Established (73.4), significantly changing the cohort's upper-band composition.

signals.title: Ukraine / Russia — May 9 Victory Day Verification Window
signals.body: Both declared ceasefires have collapsed. Ukraine reported 1,820 Russian violations within hours of its ceasefire commencement. Russia's May 8-9 Victory Day window is the next operational test. Emergency re-queue mandatory May 9-10. If Russia's own declared window collapses under documented violations, compound-escalation documentation strengthens Ukraine's restraint signal and Russia's counterparty record further. Ukraine's 50.0 Functional is held pending multi-day operational verification of either ceasefire window.

signals.title: Hungary — Magyar Swearing-In, May 9
signals.body: Peter Magyar swearing-in confirmed May 9. Pre-staged assessment dossier ready. Hold expires May 9. This is the most probable positive band-change event in the countries index in several cycles. First-day actions — state media transition, new ministry structure, EU dialogue reset, judicial independence signals — are the operative scoring inputs. Current baseline approximately 28.1 / Developing; Functional threshold at 40.0 requires substantive governance-structure evidence to cross.

signals.title: Anthropic — Phase 1 Safeguard Inventory (May 15) and DC Circuit Oral Arguments (May 19)
signals.body: Anthropic at 60.0 (Established/Functional boundary) is the frontier lab with the highest confirmed scoring volatility flag. Phase 1 safeguard inventory delivery (May 15) will test whether safeguard commitments are operationally grounded or declarative. DC Circuit oral arguments (May 19) address three pointed questions including military AI autonomy limits. Either event can produce material evidence in either direction.

signals.title: OpenAI — Musk v. Altman Advisory Verdict (Est. May 21)
signals.body: Advisory verdict expected approximately May 21. Brockman testimony has deepened the evidentiary record: $30B equity stake without capital contribution, journal admission that the for-profit conversion 'would make us liars,' first public IPO confirmation. Hold active. Breach of charitable trust finding: ACC/SYS event. Fraudulent-inducement finding: INT event. No-breach: stabilizes current range.

signals.title: DC Circuit — Anthropic Pentagon Blacklist Oral Arguments
signals.body: DC Circuit oral arguments May 19 on three questions including military AI autonomy limits. Anthropic's exclusion from the Pentagon cohort is the context. Court ruling timeline uncertain; oral arguments produce primary-source record regardless of outcome. Combined with May 15 safeguard inventory, this is a two-event window for Anthropic assessment.

signals.title: EU AI Act — GPAI Enforcement Deadline (August 2, 2026)
signals.body: 86 days to August 2, 2026 enforcement of EU AI Act GPAI model provider enforcement (fines up to 35M euros or 7% global turnover). High-risk AI provisions were delayed to December 2027 following business pressure, but GPAI enforcement activates on schedule. Palantir AI, DeepSeek, xAI/Grok, and Mistral AI have the highest documented exposure. Pre-stage compliance-status review for the ai-labs index by July 15.

signals.title: Math-Hygiene Cluster — Formula Confirmed Structural, Bulk Audit URGENT
signals.body: Cluster now at 12+ entities following tonight's additions. Robotics-labs formula diagnosis is confirmed as structural (formula-level), not entity-specific. Apptronik and 1X Technologies produced identical +8.0 discrepancies from identical dimension profiles on independent first-baseline assessments — statistical confirmation. Open Bionics has carried for 5 cycles without resolution. Data team must audit the robotics-labs composite-derivation formula before any new robotics-labs scores are published. All current robotics-labs composites should be treated as subject to this error until audit completion.

signals.title: Character.AI — Multi-State Regulatory Cascade Risk
signals.body: Pennsylvania AG characterized its May 5 enforcement action as a template. Near-term watch items: additional state AG enforcement actions following the PA model; Pennsylvania Commonwealth Court preliminary injunction outcome; any federal (FTC) engagement with the platform; any publication of a model card, system card, or safety policy by Character.AI. Any proactive safety architecture publication would be the first signal toward floor exit criteria, which currently are entirely unmet.

signals.title: Ethiopia — Floor-Designation Candidacy; 30-Day Re-Queue June 5
signals.body: Ethiopia at 4.7 with ACT, ACC, SYS, INT all at floor (1). The Pretoria Agreement architecture is de facto dissolved. Floor designation was not proposed this cycle pending documentation of federal military response and large-scale civilian harm. If federal force is deployed in response to TPLF reconstitution, or if any large-scale civilian harm event occurs in Tigray theater, an emergency re-queue before June 5 is warranted. The 30-day mandatory re-queue is June 5 regardless of event.

methodology_notes.title: Character.AI Consumer-Product Floor Designation: New Methodology Category
methodology_notes.body: Character.AI's floor designation establishes that the floor threshold in the ai-labs index can be crossed by consumer-product harm-by-design at scale without reference to autonomous weapons, state violence, or atrocity. The operative elements are: (1) documented minor mortality cluster attributable to platform interactions, (2) state-actionable statutory boundary breach (unlicensed practice of medicine) confirmed by systematic investigative search, (3) active multi-state enforcement action characterizing the conduct as novel, and (4) structural absence of any proactive safety architecture — no model card, no system card, no harm reporting mechanism, reactive-only remediation — at 20M+ user scale. The floor threshold is not exclusively consequentialist (body count at time of designation) or exclusively intentionalist (explicit directive to harm). It captures systemic harm-by-design when the combination of structural harm posture, scale, and institutional safeguard absence crosses the methodology's minimum-conduct threshold. This is the first application of that framing in the ai-labs index. Methodology version v1.2.

methodology_notes.title: Math-Hygiene Robotics-Labs Formula Diagnosis: Structural, Not Entity-Specific
methodology_notes.body: Apptronik and 1X Technologies independently produced +8.0 reconstruction discrepancies from an identical dimension profile (8 dimensions at 4.0 except EQU at 3.5) on independent first-baseline assessments tonight. Two independent assessments arriving at mathematically identical discrepancies from identical dimension inputs is diagnostic confirmation that the error is embedded in the robotics-labs index composite-derivation formula, not in any entity's individual data or assessor variance. No composite changes are proposed in the evidence cycle. All robotics-labs flags are referred to the data team per cluster protocol. The practical consequence: entities published as Exemplary (81.4) in the robotics-labs index would score Established (73.4) on a corrected formula. The bulk audit must be completed before any new robotics-labs scores are published.

methodology_notes.title: Bad-Faith-Ceasefire Rule Applied: Ukraine/Russia, May 7
methodology_notes.body: Dual ceasefire collapse documented. Ukraine reported 1,820 Russian violations within hours of its ceasefire commencement. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule, the violation signal is inverted toward Russia (counterparty), not Ukraine. Ukraine sustains 50.0 Functional. May 9-10 mandatory re-queue maintained for Victory Day verification. Ukraine's proactive ceasefire declaration and documented restraint under fire are AWR/INT positive signals at signal level, not yet composite-moving.

methodology_notes.title: Floor Cluster: Eight Entities, Four Indexes, Four Structural Categories
methodology_notes.body: With Character.AI's proposed designation, the floor cluster has eight members across four indexes: countries (Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar, Israel), ai-labs (Palantir AI, xAI/Grok, Character.AI), and robotics-labs (Ghost Robotics). The cluster now encompasses four distinct structural floor-designation categories: state-directed mass atrocity, AI weapons integration at infrastructure scale, robotics weaponization non-restriction by explicit declarative posture, and consumer AI harm-by-design at scale. The cluster's methodological diversity confirms that the floor threshold is not sector-specific and that the minimum-conduct standard applies across domains through different harm pathways.

sectorTrends.sector: AI Labs — Consumer Companion AI: New Floor Category and Regulatory Frontier
points: Character.AI's floor designation opens a new sub-category: consumer companion AI with documented harm-by-design. The distinguishing features from other ai-labs floor entities are mass consumer population including minors, product design that facilitates harm rather than enabling weaponized downstream use, and state-actionable statutory breach as the regulatory trigger.

Pennsylvania's action is characterized as a template by the AG's office. The consumer AI safety cluster (Replika, Inflection AI) should be monitored for comparable enforcement triggers. Character.AI's scale (20M+ monthly active users) distinguishes it from smaller companion-AI operators, but the methodology precedent applies regardless of scale above the floor threshold.
The FTC's consumer-protection jurisdiction over AI companion platforms is now activated as a live enforcement question following the state-level action.

sectorTrends.sector: AI Labs — Pentagon Cohort: Scored Comparable Set
points: All eight Pentagon AI cohort members are now baselined. The cohort's score range (9.4 to 37.4) reflects genuine structural differences in governance posture, not just operational scale. Cohort membership correlates with willingness to waive safety restrictions for classified military use — a scored governance variable affecting BND and INT dimensions systematically.

Anthropic's exclusion from the cohort corresponds exactly to its position as the only frontier lab above Functional band. This correlation is now documentable with precision across all eight comparison points.

Reflection AI (14.1 Critical) is the cohort's second-lowest member behind SpaceX AI (9.4 Critical floor-adjacent). Its combination of zero public model deployments, no safety policy, and politically connected governance (1789 Capital/Trump Jr.) makes it the cohort's most opaque member at the highest-risk position.

sectorTrends.sector: Robotics Labs — Commercial Humanoid Cohort: Anti-Weaponization Pledge as Structural Predictor
points: The 2022 anti-weaponization pledge is now empirically confirmed as a band predictor in the robotics-labs index. All signatories with baselines (Boston Dynamics, Agility) score Established or above. Non-signatories (Figure AI, Tesla Optimus, Ghost Robotics) score Functional or below. The pledge is a verifiable BND and INT dimension signal.

The ASTM humanoid safety standards panel (Boston Dynamics, Agility, Figure AI — meeting May 27-28 in Boston) is the next policy event with potential BND dimension implications. Figure AI's participation without pledge signature is a split-signal worth tracking.

The math-hygiene formula error, when corrected, would move Apptronik and 1X Technologies from Exemplary to Established. This would leave Open Bionics as the only Exemplary-band robotics-labs entity and fundamentally change the index's upper-band distribution.

sectorTrends.sector: Countries — Eastern Europe: Constitutional Regression Cluster
points: Slovakia's first-baseline designation (Functional → Developing) adds to an emerging EU-member constitutional regression cluster. Unlike the prior week's Established → Functional cohort (Mauritius, Spain, UK), Slovakia arrives at the index already in Developing band — indicating regression embedded before baseline, not drift from a higher position.

The EU accountability scaffolding (ICCPR Committee, ECtHR, NHRI) detected Slovakia's constitutional rollback but has not produced compliance pressure sufficient to halt it. This is an ACC dimension observation with systemic implications for EU oversight effectiveness.
Hungary's May 9 swearing-in is the potential counterpoint in the cluster — the first positive band-change opportunity in the EU-member index in several cycles.

keyHighlights: Character.AI is the eighth floor entity and the first designated for consumer-product harm-by-design: chatbots impersonating licensed psychiatrists at scale, a minor-fatality settlement, and total absence of proactive safety architecture at 20M+ user scale. The Pennsylvania AG's first-of-its-kind enforcement action is the documentary anchor. This is a methodology-defining designation.

Ghost Robotics (May 6) and Character.AI (May 7) are the first consecutive-day floor designations in the pipeline's history. The cluster now spans four indexes and four distinct structural harm categories. The floor threshold is confirmed as applicable across sectors through different harm pathways.

Apptronik and 1X Technologies independently produced identical +8.0 reconstruction discrepancies on tonight's first-baseline assessments, confirming a structural formula error in the robotics-labs index composite derivation. The bulk data-team audit of the robotics-labs formula is now a confirmed structural priority, not an optional backlog item.

The Pentagon AI cohort is fully baselined for the first time: all eight signatory labs have agent assessments. No cohort member is above Functional band. Anthropic — excluded from the cohort — is the only frontier lab above Functional. The correlation between cohort exclusion and higher score is now documentable with dimension-level precision.

Slovakia's first-baseline designation (Functional → Developing) on a September 2025 constitutional amendment erasing legal gender recognition signals a deeper-than-boundary-drift regression: the country arrived at its first assessment already below its published placeholder score. TIAA's material downgrade from Fortune 500 rank 1 (58.6 → 48.4) on 16 years of ERISA self-dealing is the most significant Fortune 500 scoring event of the current cycle.
